events.title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
events.description: Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday.

events.title: Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says
events.description: Almost every child, including those from high-income countries, is now exposed to at least one hazard Half of the world’s children are exposed to at least three overlapping climate hazards threatening their health, education and survival, according to a Unicef report. Globally, children face increasing threats from heatwaves, storms, floods and droughts as the climate crisis worsens, with more than one billion facing at least three of these at once. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Half of world’s children exposed to at least three climate hazards, Unicef says

events.title: Violent attacks on schools, pupils and staff around the world up by 40%, says study
events.description: Cases reported in 83 countries, with at least 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted or arrested, GCPEA says Attacks on education globally have surged by 40% with more than 8,556 recorded incidents and 10,600 students and staff killed, injured, abducted, arrested or otherwise harmed in 2024 and 2025, according to new research. Attacks were reported in 83 countries, with the highest incidences recorded in Colombia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, Haiti, Palestine and Ukraine. Continue reading...

events.title: Deadly Philippines earthquake found to have raised seabed by up to 2 metres
events.description: ‘Coastal uplift’ exposes coral and kills marine life, as residents say shorelines extended by up to 200 metres A powerful earthquake that killed at least 61 people in the Philippines this week raised the seabed by as much as 2 metres (6.6ft), exposing coral and harming marine life, the country’s environment department has said. At least 40 people are still missing after the 7.8-magnitude quake off southern Mindanao island on Monday, according to updated tolls from the disaster agency. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Deadly Philippines earthquake found to have raised seabed by up to 2 metres

events.title: Social media becomes world’s leading news source, report warns
events.description: News consumers worldwide are increasingly turning to social media and video platforms for information, overtaking traditional outlets for the first time, according to a Reuters Institute report. The study warns that shifting habits, driven by younger audiences and AI tools, are reshaping the global news industry and threatening old media business models.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Social media becomes world’s leading news source, report warns

events.title: World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe
events.description: Violence spread across Belfast following a shocking knife attack allegedly carried out by a Sudanese asylum seeker on Monday, triggering a wave of anti-immigration unrest. The victim suffered significant injuries to his face and back.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe

events.title: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
events.description: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts

events.title: Iran war 'has been a strategic disaster for the State of Israel', analyst says
events.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is on a collision course with Donald Trump as the U.S. president seeks to extricate himself from the war, with both men's goals unmet and Israeli military operations tied down in Lebanon. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Aaron David Miller, Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, says that "this has been a strategic disaster for the state of Israel. All of Benjamin Netanyahu's war aims - diminished support for proxies, restrictions on ballistic missiles, overthrow of the regime, or weakening of the regime - none of this has occurred."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war 'has been a strategic disaster for the State of Israel', analyst says

events.title: Ukraine: Latest Russian attack kills civilians, damages cultural landmark
events.description: Several civilians were killed and dozens more were injured in the latest wave of overnight attacks in Ukraine that targeted the capital Kyiv, the city of Kharkiv and the country’s history and cultural heritage, the United Nations said on Monday.

events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks
events.description: A Guardian analysis reveals how most of 39 countries facing US entry restrictions are most vulnerable environmentally ‘Every day it’s more barriers’: how the US is shutting out climate refugees Donald Trump ’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows. As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels , millions of people are being forced to flee their homelands due to storms, floods and droughts worsened by the climate crisis . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks

events.title: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
events.description: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children

events.title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children
events.description: Drought, extreme heat and heatwaves are the most prevalent trio of hazards endangering millions of children globally, warned a newly released climate report by the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children

events.title: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
events.description: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

events.title: Christian leaders hold emergency summit in Jerusalem to confront global rise in antisemitism
events.description: International Christian Embassy Jerusalem is hosting an emergency summit addressing rising antisemitism and replacement theology amid church leaders' silence.

events.title: Middle East live: Iranian FM says talks to finalise agreement with US 'likely' to start Friday
events.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Tuesday that talks on a final agreement with the United States covering Tehran's nuclear programme among other points will likely begin on Friday. Araghchi also warned that ‌any Israeli attack on Lebanon or continued presence ‌on Lebanese territory constituted a violation of the interim agreement with the United States. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East live: Iranian FM says talks to finalise agreement with US 'likely' to start Friday

events.title: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
events.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation

events.title: Sri Lanka sees ‘alarming’ rise in cybercrime as scam networks relocate from south-east Asia
events.description: Experts say criminal networks favour Sri Lanka due to ease of getting tourist visas and limited regulation on sim cards and internet connections Experts have warned that Sri Lanka is emerging as a hub for transnational cybercrime, after a crackdown in south-east Asia pushed Chinese-run criminal networks to relocate their vast scam operations. Sri Lankan police spokesperson Fredrick Wootler said the country was witnessing an “alarming increase of cybercrimes” perpetrated by people entering the country as tourists, and then illegally setting up scam operations targeting people across the world. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sri Lanka sees ‘alarming’ rise in cybercrime as scam networks relocate from south-east Asia

events.title: Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct
events.description: Adam Castillo, founder of AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport. The U.S. State Department is aware of a reported detention but hasn't commented further.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct

events.title: 8 people dead after B-52 bomber crashes at US military base
events.description: The aircraft crashed at California's Edwards Air Force base. An investigation is now underway into the cause of the disaster.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ 8 people dead after B-52 bomber crashes at US military base

events.title: World News in Brief: State-supported safe houses in Haiti, EU pact strengthens refugee protection, demand for ‘critical minerals’ intensifies
events.description: Haiti has opened its first State-supported safe house for survivors of sexual violence with support from the UN, marking a significant step in responding to the growing crisis affecting women and girls.

events.title: U.S. and Iran announce an initial deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The deal is a major breakthrough in the conflict that set the Middle East aflame and shook the global economy. However, it did not resolve critical issues set aside for further negotiations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. and Iran announce an initial deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

events.title: UK’s ban on Palestine Action was lawful, Court of Appeal says
events.description: The British government acted lawfully when it banned the protest group Palestine Action as a terrorist organization, the Court of Appeal in London has ruled
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK’s ban on Palestine Action was lawful, Court of Appeal says

events.title: Two arrested during investigation into Dezi Freeman’s movements as police reveal killer likely travelled to NSW
events.description: Victoria and NSW police search seven properties as they examine where shooter travelled after killing two officers in Porepunkah Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two more men have been arrested as police continue their investigation into Porepunkah shooter Dezi Freeman’s months on the run after he killed two police officers in August 2025. In a statement on Tuesday, police also revealed they believed Freeman travelled between Victoria and New South Wales in the seven months between the shootings at Porepunkah and when he was found on a remote property in Thologolong, near Walwa on the Victoria-New South Wales border, on 30 March. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Two arrested during investigation into Dezi Freeman’s movements as police reveal killer likely travelled to NSW

events.title: France shuts down twelve Israeli stands at defence trade show
events.description: A dozen Israeli company stands were shut down Monday at a major international defence and security exhibition held outside Paris, sparking outrage from authorities in Israel. Tensions have persisted in French-Israeli relations, with France recognising Palestinian statehood last year and two far-right Israeli ministers banned from entering the country in recent weeks. FRANCE 24's Jennie Shin reports.

events.title: Iran held by New Zealand in World Cup opener overshadowed by politics
events.description: Iran twice came from behind to salvage a 2–2 draw against New Zealand in a World Cup opener overshadowed by geopolitical tensions, protests and months of conflict with the United States. The result leaves Team Melli still searching for a breakthrough on football's biggest stage.

events.title: US B-52 bomber crash at California air force base kills eight
events.description: Eight people were killed after a US B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after take-off from Edwards Air Force Base in California on Monday. The aircraft was carrying a mix of military personnel, government employees and civilian contractors on a routine test mission when it went down, prompting an immediate investigation.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ US B-52 bomber crash at California air force base kills eight

events.title: Iran war is a 'wake-up call' for Southeast Asia's energy sector, report says
events.description: The International Energy Agency says Southeast Asia’s power sector is too open to risks from its dependence on imports of oil and gas from a limited number of suppliers
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war is a 'wake-up call' for Southeast Asia's energy sector, report says

events.title: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
events.description: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears

events.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
events.description: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

events.title: Egypt draw 1–1 with Belgium at World Cup group game
events.description: Emam Ashour's first international goal gave Egypt the lead, before veteran Romelu Lukaku forced an own goal to equalise.

events.title: Lead negotiator on 2015 Iran nuclear deal weighs in on what's next for Trump's deal
events.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Wendy Sherman, the lead U.S. negotiator on the 2015 Iran nuclear agreement, about the road ahead for U.S. negotiators aiming to strike a new deal to end the Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lead negotiator on 2015 Iran nuclear deal weighs in on what's next for Trump's deal

events.title: UN calls for drone regulation in conflict zones
events.description: UN rights chief Volker Türk said more than 1,000 civilians in Sudan have been killed this year by drone attacks. The UN has called for regulation and accountability to prevent similar atrocities.

events.title: 'First line of defence' has collapsed in biggest ever outbreak of rare Ebola strain
events.description: The number of Ebola infections in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is likely far higher than official figures, Oxfam has warned, due to lack of clean water and sanitation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'First line of defence' has collapsed in biggest ever outbreak of rare Ebola strain

events.title: Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin
events.description: Ibo Maza had his choice of three different countries. Instead of his native Germany, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is representing Algeria, the country of his father, at the World Cup in North America.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ibrahim Maza: Algeria's 'multicultural kid' from Berlin

events.title: Iran deal: Trump is back to square one, but the cards are now in Tehran’s favor
events.description: The signing of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between the US and Iran on Monday has been welcomed by the international community after a devastating three-month war. The agreement was announced on June 14, President Donald Trump’s birthday, but the biggest beneficiary, experts say, is the Iranian regime.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran deal: Trump is back to square one, but the cards are now in Tehran’s favor

events.title: World News in Brief: Risky return home in Lebanon, displacement in Gaza, emergency funding for Somalia
events.description: Despite the agreement announced on Sunday between the United States and Iran, displaced people in Lebanon continue to face difficulties in returning to their communities, the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has reported.

events.title: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
events.description: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze

events.title: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
events.description: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global Witness Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims. The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied by the M23 militia , which has committed myriad atrocities in eastern DRC. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds

events.title: BoM forecasts strong El Niño and warns climate change could amplify any effects on Australia
events.description: El Niño events linked with extreme weather around the world – and can increase risk of bushfires in Australia and coral bleaching on Great Barrier Reef Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Bureau of Meteorology has officially declared an El Niño – the phenomenon linked to hotter and drier conditions for Australia – is now locked in place in the tropical Pacific Ocean. The bureau warned climate change would amplify the effects on Australia, including the risk of extreme heat and bushfires. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ BoM forecasts strong El Niño and warns climate change could amplify any effects on Australia

events.title: National Guardsman pleads guilty to fatal shooting of soldier he found in bed with his ex-girlfriend
events.description: A U.S. Army National Guardsman pleaded guilty to killing a soldier he found in bed with his ex-girlfriend at Fort Gordon in Augusta, Georgia.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 National Guardsman pleads guilty to fatal shooting of soldier he found in bed with his ex-girlfriend

events.title: US-Iran deal leaves major Lebanon questions unresolved
events.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Negah Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London and Former Senior Advisor at the US Department of State and National Security Council under the Biden Administration, says that "the real test is going to be whether or not this framework survives the Lebanon angle and whether it can, on the American side, be translated into some sort of nuclear framework".

events.title: Officials in Brazil investigate helicopter crash that killed 6
events.description: Officials in Brazil were inspecting the site of a Sunday's fatal helicopter crash

events.title: Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict

events.ticker_label: 🌪️ American hiker vanishes on tropical trek as flash floods, mudslides turn jungle getaway into desperate search

events.title: Mexican authorities discover body in trunk near Iranian soccer team's World Cup training grounds: report
events.description: Mexican authorities found a decomposing corpse with signs of violence near Tijuana's Caliente Stadium, where Iran's team trains for the World Cup.

events.title: G7 leaders open summit talks in France on Ukraine and the Middle East

events.title: At least 31 dead after Ethiopia bus crash
events.description: At least ‌31 people have died and 33 others were injured after a bus ‌plunged into a ravine ​in Ethiopia, police said.

events.title: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal

events.title: US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?
events.description: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?

events.title: France kick off World Cup campaign, with Senegal looking to repeat 2002 upset
events.description: Les Bleus will be wary of carrying the favourites tag when they begin their quest for a third World Cup title against Senegal on Tuesday, 24 years after their shock defeat against the Lions of Teranga set off dancing in the streets of Dakar – and precipitated France's calamitous exit in the group stage.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Belgium rescue point against Egypt in Group G thriller
events.description: No description available.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Lukaku comes off the bench to rescue point for Belgium against Egypt
events.description: Belgium's Romelo Lukaku came off the bench to help Belgium level against Egypt in a thrilling Group G clash at the Seattle Stadium on Monday, after midfielder Emam Ashour had put the Pharaohs ahead with a superb shot. The draw leaves the seven-time African champions still chasing their first victory on football's biggest stage.

events.title: Oil prices drop and stocks enjoy cautious rally on US-Iran peace progress

events.title: US musician Oliver Tree, 32, killed in helicopter crash in Brazil
events.description: Alternative singer and internet personality among six who died when two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro The American musician Oliver Tree has died in a helicopter crash in Brazil at the age of 32, according to reports. Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday morning and crashed in the city’s western zone, killing all six people onboard, including Tree, several Brazilian media outlets reported. Continue reading...

events.description: The earthquake hit part of Indonesia's Sulawesi island. Indonesia lies on the Pacific "Ring of Fire" and is therefore prone to frequent, strong earthquakes.

events.title: Refugee turned peacemaker: The young woman leader transforming communities in the Central African Republic

events.title: Ukraine civilian casualty toll in May highest in four years, UN monitors say
events.description: More civilians were killed and injured in Ukraine in May than in any other month in the past four years, UN investigators said on Friday in their latest update.

events.title: WHO report shows progress in blood safety, but there are worrying gaps
events.description: Every day, safe blood helps save the lives of women experiencing childbirth complications, accident victims, cancer patients and people living with chronic diseases. Yet despite decades of progress, access to lifesaving blood remains deeply unequal, with shortages continuing to put lives at risk in many lower-income countries, according to a new World Health Organization (WHO) report.

events.title: Russia linked to arson attacks on properties connected to UK PM Keir Starmer, police say
events.description: Russia allegedly linked to arson attacks on U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer's properties, with a reported diplomat accused of recruiting Ukrainian nationals.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside

events.title: Germany news: 23 million tune in for World Cup win

events.title: Interim US-Iran peace deal sparks anger among Israelis, who lash out at Netanyahu
events.description: Israelis from across the political spectrum reacted angrily Monday to the news of an initial deal between the U.S. and Iran, calling it a disaster for Israel and directing their fury at one man: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

events.title: London council seizes social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone first lady
events.description: Fatima Jabbe-Bio kept tenancy in Southwark despite living for much of year at presidential lodge in Freetown A social housing flat rented by Sierra Leone’s first lady has been seized by a London council. Southwark council confirmed it had repossessed the two-bedroom home in Walworth previously occupied by Fatima Jabbe-Bio, whose tenancy was reported by the Times last year. Continue reading...

events.title: Starbucks Korea to temporarily shut all stores for history lesson after bungled coffee promotion
events.description: The closures, so employees can watch a recorded lecture, will cost the company an estimated 2.1bn won ($1.4m) in sales Starbucks Korea will simultaneously close all its stores for a mandatory history lesson, after a disastrous promotion that evoked memories of a pro-democracy massacre sparked public and political backlash . More than 2,000 stores will temporarily close at 3pm on 22 June, the company said, so staff can watch recorded lectures on modern Korean history and engage in “social sensitivity” training. The half-day closures will cost Starbucks an estimated 2.1bn won ($1.4m) in lost sales, according to data firm IGAWorks. Continue reading...

events.title: Mourners line Bangkok streets to pay respects to Thailand’s Princess Bha
events.description: Funeral procession travels to palace as people remember royal’s campaigning and work for underprivileged As the sun began to set on the golden spires and gilded finials of Bangkok’s Grand Palace, the gates were open, waiting for the return of a princess. Since December 2022, Princess Bajrakitiyabha had been in hospital, having collapsed while out training her dogs. After nearly four years in a coma, the princess died earlier this week . events.title: Two arrested as police investigate Dezi Freeman’s movements – as it happened
events.description: This is blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Gallagher on One Nation: ‘Opinion polls will come and go’ Katy Gallagher was also asked about the surge in One Nation, saying there was an obvious “reorganisation on the conservative side of politics”. I think there is a lot of uncertainty and worry about events that are happening across the world, the impact that has here. Cost of living is the No 1 issue with anyone you talk to right now, and the government just has to remain focused on that, and that’s what we intend to do. Opinion polls will come and go, they do. Our job is to make sure we’re making the right decisions for the right time and that it’s focused on the Australian people. And when there’s controversy about that, and we know we’ve had a lot of feedback around the budget, you know, our job is to front up and explain why we’ve taken those decisions. Continue reading...

events.title: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies

Almeida, 95, was the president of the Mothers of Plaza de Mayo, made up of women who have marched around the square outside Argentina’s presidential palace every Thursday since 1977 , demanding the return of children who were disappeared during the country’s 1976-1983 dictatorship. events.title: Trump arrives in France for G7 leaders summit, touting peace deal with Iran
events.description: French president Emmanuel Macron met with Trump and congratulated him for signing the new Iran agreement.

events.title: Crude oil is cheaper as markets embrace news of a U.S.-Iran deal
events.description: Crude oil prices are falling as oil markets react to President Trump saying the U.S. and Iran have agreed on a framework for reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge in nod to Christo opens in Paris
events.description: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

events.title: California Governor Newsom says being investigated by Trump's administration
events.description: Trump's political rival Gavin Newsom said that Trump is targeting him and his wife by investigating them, without specifying the allegations. The California governor has locked horns with Trump on several occasions.

events.title: Iran updates: Netanyahu refrains from criticizing Iran deal
events.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu did say he doesn't always share the same views as US President Donald Trump. DW has the latest.

events.title: UK announces social media ban for under-16s
events.description: The United Kingdom is set to follow the example of Australia and Indonesia by banning teenagers from social networks. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said platforms like Instagram and TikTok make youngsters "unhappy."

events.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
events.description: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

events.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
events.description: There are still mines in the Straight of Hormuz, delaying the opening for commercial shipping. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

events.title: The potential of geothermal energy for US homes
events.description: Advancements in geothermal systems and sustained political support in the US provide an opportunity for this clean energy industry to scale.

events.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince jailed for two rapes
events.description: The stepson of Norway's Crown Prince Haakon has been jailed for two rapes - including one in the basement of his family's home.

events.title: South Africa marks 50 years since Soweto uprising, but challenges linger
events.description: South Africa marks the 50th anniversary of the Soweto uprising, a pivotal moment in the fight against apartheid

events.title: Rare first edition of 'Wuthering Heights' with spelling mistakes is up for auction
events.description: A rare first-edition copy of Emily Brontë’s novel "Wuthering Heights" is up for auction for the first time in over a century

events.title: Syrian sentenced to 26 years in prison by Dutch court for crimes against humanity
events.description: A Dutch court has convicted a Syrian man of crimes against humanity, sentencing him to 26 years in prison for torturing and raping opponents of former President Bashar Assad in 2013-14

events.title: Greek minister calls criticism of tougher migration policies a ‘badge of honor’
events.description: Greece’s migration minister calls criticism from human rights groups a “badge of honor” and plans to toughen migration policies

events.title: Ukraine starts EU membership talks and faces years of reforms while fighting Russia
events.description: Ukraine has officially started European Union membership negotiations, a long process that requires political reforms while facing a Russian invasion
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine starts EU membership talks and faces years of reforms while fighting Russia

events.title: Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct
events.description: The former president of the American Chamber of Commerce in Myanmar has been detained in Yangon

events.title: Teen accused of killing stepsister on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained before murder trial
events.description: Teen accused of killing stepsister Anna Kepner on Carnival cruise ship ordered detained after judge finds no release conditions can protect community.

events.title: Austin Metcalf's father rips former Frisco schools chief as 'spineless' over Karmelo Anthony graduation
events.description: Father of slain teen Austin Metcalf slams former Frisco ISD superintendent, claiming he allowed killer Karmelo Anthony to graduate after the stabbing.

events.title: Six men allegedly kidnapped two people at vacation resort, tortured them at boat ramp: FBI
events.description: Six men allegedly kidnapped two people at gunpoint from Margaritaville Hollywood Beach Resort in Florida, tortured them and demanded money, officials say.

events.title: Stephen A. Smith says 'Karmelo Anthony murdered Austin Metcalf'
events.description: Stephen A. Smith says Karmelo Anthony murdered Austin Metcalf, challenging self-defense claims after the 35-year sentence for the Frisco stabbing.

events.title: President Trump announces Iran peace agreement and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Detroit sisters accused of stabbing restaurant worker after wrong food order
events.description: Two Michigan sisters, including one who was nine months pregnant, allegedly stabbed a worker at a restaurant and threw hot oil at her over a wrong order.

events.title: Boston police arrest 14-year-old after masked suspects allegedly rob siblings’ lemonade stand
events.description: A 14-year-old suspect was arrested in connection with an armed robbery that allegedly targeted a children's lemonade stand in South Boston last week.

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.
